name: ADS-B Ultrafeeder
description: All-in-one ADS-B solution: readsb decoder, tar1090 map, graphs1090 stats, autogain, multi-feeder, and MLAT hub in a single container.

config_fields.label: SDR device
config_fields.help: Serial of the RTL-SDR to use. Leave blank to auto-select the first one.

config_fields.label: Gain
config_fields.help: Receiver gain, or 'autogain'.

config_fields.label: Latitude
config_fields.help: Antenna latitude (decimal degrees).

config_fields.label: Longitude
config_fields.help: Antenna longitude (decimal degrees).

long_description: ADS-B Ultrafeeder is the recommended all-in-one ADS-B install for most Airwaves OS receivers. It bundles readsb, tar1090, graphs1090, adaptive gain, Beast/RAW network outputs, and feeder integrations in one managed container.
install_notes: Best choice when this SDR is dedicated to 1090 MHz ADS-B.
Uses host ports 8080, 30003, and 30005; stop readsb or tar1090 first if those ports are already in use.
Set latitude and longitude to enable range plots, MLAT-ready output, and accurate maps.

outputs.label: ADS-B aircraft positions
outputs.description: Decoded aircraft position and Mode S traffic from 1090 MHz.

outputs.label: tar1090 map
outputs.description: Interactive aircraft map exposed on port 8080.

outputs.label: Beast output
outputs.description: Beast-format ADS-B stream for local consumers.

suggested_feeds.name: Airplanes.live ADS-B
suggested_feeds.description: Optional ADS-B feed destination using Ultrafeeder output.

suggested_feeds.name: Custom ADS-B Beast Output
suggested_feeds.description: Forward decoded ADS-B Beast output to a custom host.

name: readsb
description: ADS-B Mode S/Mode AC decoder and feed server. Receives 1090 MHz signals from aircraft transponders. Use Ultrafeeder instead for an all-in-one solution.

config_fields.label: SDR device
config_fields.help: Serial of the RTL-SDR to use. Leave blank to auto-select the first one.

config_fields.label: Gain
config_fields.help: Receiver gain, or 'autogain'.

config_fields.label: Latitude
config_fields.help: Antenna latitude (decimal degrees).

config_fields.label: Longitude
config_fields.help: Antenna longitude (decimal degrees).

long_description: readsb is a focused ADS-B Mode S decoder and network server. Install it when you want a lean decoder feeding another map, aggregator, or local application instead of the full Ultrafeeder stack.
install_notes: Do not run readsb and Ultrafeeder together unless you change overlapping host ports.
Set receiver location if you plan to use range graphs, MLAT, or downstream maps.

outputs.label: ADS-B aircraft positions
outputs.description: Decoded aircraft and Mode S traffic.

outputs.label: readsb web UI
outputs.description: Built-in receiver view exposed on port 8078.

suggested_feeds.name: Airplanes.live ADS-B
suggested_feeds.description: Optional ADS-B feed destination from readsb Beast output.

suggested_feeds.name: Custom ADS-B Beast Output
suggested_feeds.description: Forward decoded ADS-B Beast output to a custom host.

name: acarsdec
description: Multi-channel ACARS decoder. Receives VHF ACARS messages on frequencies around 130-136 MHz and outputs decoded text for feeding to ACARS Hub or aggregators.

config_fields.label: SDR device
config_fields.help: Select which connected SDR acarsdec should use.

config_fields.label: Frequencies (MHz)
config_fields.help: Semicolon-separated VHF ACARS frequencies, e.g. 130.025;130.450;131.125;131.550

config_fields.label: Feed ID
config_fields.help: Optional station/feed identifier sent with messages.

config_fields.label: Gain (dB)
config_fields.help: Receiver gain. Leave default for automatic.

long_description: acarsdec decodes classic VHF ACARS messages from aircraft. It is useful as a lightweight source feeding ACARS Router, ACARS Hub, or a direct UDP/TCP destination.
install_notes: Choose one SDR and confirm its antenna is suitable for VHF airband.
The default frequency set covers common ACARS channels; adjust for your region.
Install ACARS Hub or ACARS Router if you want a local message viewer or forwarding pipeline.

suggested_feeds.name: Custom ACARS Output
suggested_feeds.description: Forward ACARS messages to a custom host.

name: dumpvdl2
description: VDL Mode 2 decoder. Receives VHF Data Link Mode 2 messages used for ACARS-over-AVLC digital datalink between aircraft and ground stations.

config_fields.label: SDR device
config_fields.help: Select which connected SDR this app should use.

config_fields.label: Frequencies (MHz)
config_fields.help: Semicolon-separated VDL2 frequencies.

long_description: dumpvdl2 decodes VDL Mode 2 aircraft datalink traffic, including ACARS-over-AVLC and CPDLC-related frames. It is a higher-rate companion to classic ACARS monitoring.
install_notes: Use an SDR and antenna tuned for VHF airband.
The default VDL2 channels are common but regional channel use varies.
Enable ACARS Router or ACARS Hub when you want a live searchable message view.

suggested_feeds.name: Custom VDL2 Output
suggested_feeds.description: Forward VDL2 messages to a custom host.

name: dumphfdl
description: HFDL decoder. Receives High Frequency Data Link messages used for long-range oceanic aircraft communications on HF bands (2-22 MHz).

config_fields.label: SDR device
config_fields.help: Select the SDR (an Airspy HF+ or wideband SDR is recommended for HF).

long_description: dumphfdl decodes High Frequency Data Link messages used by aircraft on long-haul oceanic and remote routes. It works best with an HF-capable receiver and antenna.
install_notes: HF reception requires the right antenna and SDR; Airspy HF+ or similar hardware is recommended.
Pick the SDR explicitly if multiple radios are connected.

suggested_feeds.name: Custom HFDL Output
suggested_feeds.description: Forward HFDL messages to a custom host.

name: ACARS Router
description: Message router for ACARS, VDL2, and HFDL data. Receives decoded messages from multiple decoders and routes them to ACARS Hub, aggregators, and other outputs.

config_fields.label: Send ACARS to (UDP host:port)
config_fields.help: Optional UDP target for ACARS JSON, e.g. acarshub:5550.

config_fields.label: Send VDLM2 to (UDP host:port)
config_fields.help: Optional UDP target for VDLM2 JSON, e.g. acarshub:5555.

long_description: ACARS Router ties multiple aircraft datalink decoders together. Use it to receive ACARS, VDL2, or HFDL data locally and route normalized messages to ACARS Hub or external destinations.
install_notes: Install this after at least one ACARS, VDL2, or HFDL decoder.
Use container hostnames like airwaves-acarshub for local Airwaves apps on the shared app network.

outputs.label: Routed datalink messages
outputs.description: Normalized ACARS, VDL2, and HFDL output.

suggested_feeds.name: Local ACARS Hub
suggested_feeds.description: Route local decoded messages to ACARS Hub.

name: AIS-Catcher
description: AIS receiver and decoder. Captures Automatic Identification System signals from ships and maritime vessels on 161.975 and 162.025 MHz.

config_fields.label: SDR device
config_fields.help: Serial of the RTL-SDR to use for AIS reception.

long_description: AIS-Catcher receives AIS position reports from ships on the marine VHF AIS channels and exposes a local web view plus network output for maritime feeders.
install_notes: Use a VHF antenna suitable for 162 MHz marine AIS.
The web UI is exposed on port 8100 after install.

suggested_feeds.name: Custom AIS Output
suggested_feeds.description: Forward AIS messages to a custom host.

name: rtl_airband
description: Multi-channel AM/FM demodulator for airband frequencies. Streams live audio from aviation voice channels (Tower, Ground, Approach) via Icecast.

config_fields.label: SDR device
config_fields.help: Serial of the RTL-SDR to use. Leave blank if only one is connected.

config_fields.label: Gain
config_fields.help: Dongle gain (dB).

long_description: rtl_airband demodulates aviation voice channels and publishes live audio streams, usually through Icecast. Airwaves OS adds an Airband page when this app is installed so operators can monitor channels and stream endpoints.
install_notes: Installs the rtl_airband container and unlocks the bundled Airband page in Airwaves OS.
Use a VHF airband antenna and an SDR dedicated to voice monitoring.
The default stream port is 8000.

outputs.label: Bundled Airband page
outputs.description: Airwaves OS control page for rtl_airband status, streams, config, and logs.

suggested_feeds.name: Local Airband Audio Stream
suggested_feeds.description: Track the local Icecast/audio stream endpoint in Feeds.

value_page.label: Airband
value_page.description: Bundled Airwaves OS page unlocked by rtl_airband for stream status, configuration, and logs.

bundled_features.label: Airband
bundled_features.description: Adds the Airband navigation item and live rtl_airband management page when installed.

name: rtl_433
description: Generic data receiver for ISM band devices. Decodes weather stations, tire pressure sensors, doorbells, and hundreds of other 433/868/915 MHz devices.

config_fields.label: SDR device
config_fields.help: Serial of the RTL-SDR to use (rtl_433 selects the device by serial).

long_description: rtl_433 decodes short-range ISM-band devices such as weather stations, tire-pressure sensors, utility meters, and simple remote sensors.
install_notes: Choose the SDR assigned to ISM-band monitoring.
The default command emits JSON logs; use container logs or a downstream collector to consume events.

suggested_feeds.name: Custom rtl_433 JSON Output
suggested_feeds.description: Forward rtl_433 JSON events to a local collector.

name: SatDump
description: Satellite data processing suite. Decodes weather satellite imagery and data from NOAA, Meteor-M, GOES, Himawari, and other satellites.

config_fields.label: SDR device
config_fields.help: Select which connected SDR this app should use.

long_description: SatDump is a broad satellite decoding and processing suite for weather and data satellites. It is best used with app-specific satellite plans and an SDR/antenna suited to the target satellite band.
install_notes: Use the right SDR and antenna for the satellite family you want to receive.
The web interface is exposed on port 8081 when the image provides it.

outputs.label: Satellite data products
outputs.description: Decoded imagery and telemetry files produced by SatDump.

name: dump978
description: UAT 978 MHz decoder for US ADS-B. Receives traffic from aircraft using the Universal Access Transceiver system (US only, below 18,000 ft).

config_fields.label: SDR device
config_fields.help: Serial of the RTL-SDR to use for 978 MHz UAT. Leave blank to auto-select.

long_description: dump978 receives UAT traffic on 978 MHz, including ADS-B traffic and FIS-B weather products in the United States.
install_notes: Use this only where 978 MHz UAT is active, primarily the United States.
Use a separate SDR from any 1090 MHz ADS-B receiver.

outputs.label: 978 MHz UAT messages
outputs.description: Decoded UAT traffic and weather products.

suggested_feeds.name: Custom UAT Output
suggested_feeds.description: Forward decoded UAT output to a custom host.

name: ACARS Hub
description: Web-based ACARS message viewer and aggregator. Displays decoded ACARS, VDL2, and HFDL messages in a searchable web interface with statistics and maps.

config_fields.label: Enable ACARS
config_fields.help: Set 'true' to ingest ACARS.

config_fields.label: Enable VDLM2
config_fields.help: Set 'true' to ingest VDLM2.

long_description: ACARS Hub is the local web viewer for decoded ACARS, VDL2, and HFDL traffic. It is most useful after installing one or more aircraft datalink decoders or ACARS Router.
install_notes: Install ACARS, VDL2, or HFDL decoders first if you want live data immediately.
The web UI is exposed on port 8900.

name: tar1090
description: Web-based aircraft tracking map. Displays real-time aircraft positions from ADS-B data on an interactive map with history trails and range rings.

config_fields.label: Beast source host
config_fields.help: Container providing Beast data (e.g. airwaves-readsb or airwaves-ultrafeeder).

long_description: tar1090 is an aircraft map UI that consumes Beast data from readsb or Ultrafeeder. Install it when you want a dedicated web map separate from the decoder container.
install_notes: Requires a running Beast source such as airwaves-readsb or airwaves-ultrafeeder.
The web map is exposed on port 8082.

name: Ship Feeder
description: AIS data feeder based on AIS-Catcher. Feeds ship tracking data to multiple aggregators including MarineTraffic, VesselFinder, and others.

config_fields.label: AIS-Catcher UDP inputs
config_fields.help: UDP feed inputs, e.g. airwaves-ais-catcher:4002.

long_description: Ship Feeder forwards AIS data from AIS-Catcher or another AIS source to maritime aggregators. It is a feeder/connector, not a decoder by itself.
install_notes: Install AIS-Catcher first or provide another AIS UDP input.
Configure the UDP input source before enabling external forwarding.

outputs.label: Forwarded AIS vessel data
outputs.description: AIS data prepared for maritime aggregators.

suggested_feeds.name: MarineTraffic AIS
suggested_feeds.description: Track the MarineTraffic AIS feed configured through Ship Feeder.

suggested_feeds.name: Custom AIS Feed
suggested_feeds.description: Forward AIS output to a custom destination.
